title: Human openings ground truth
description: Machine-readable mandatory ground truth for openings (doors, windows, glazed balconies). When this file exists, it overrides any detector-emitted opening for the same planta. Extracted from a color-coded PNG annotation by `tools/extract_human_openings.py`.

source_image.description: Relative path to the annotated PNG.

color_mapping.description: RGB → opening kind mapping used during extraction.

required_counts.description: Total count of openings of each kind. The structural_checks gate FAILS if any actual count falls below the required.

openings.description: Extracted opening list. Each entry is the canonical anchor for `consume_consensus.rb` to render.

wall_id.description: Nearest matching wall id from consensus.walls. May be null if no wall is close enough; the apply step will then create a soft-anchor opening with `wall_id=null` (the Ruby exporter handles these as floating door leaves).

explicit_constraints.description: Named positional constraints (e.g. 'BANHO 02 must have west door'). Each is enforced as its own gate; failing any causes FAIL.

search_region_pts.description: [x0, y0, x1, y1] in PDF points. For require_present: at least one opening of `kind` must have its center inside this region. For require_absent: no opening of `kind` may have its center inside this region.
